questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
questions.explanation: Same perimeter can give different areas

questions.question: Find the area of a square whose diagonal is {tex}10\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm.

Area = 10² = 100 cm²

questions.question:
A triangle and a parallelogram have the same base and same height.
The area of the triangle is 20 cm².
The area of the parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
Parallelogram = base × height
Triangle = ½ × base × height

The diagonals of a rhombus are in the ratio 3 : 4.

questions.explanation: Any polygon can be divided into triangles.

b = 12 cm

questions.question:
Two triangles have the same base and lie between the same parallel lines.

questions.question: Find the area of a trapezium whose parallel sides are 10 cm and 14 cm and height is 5 cm.

parallel sides {tex}=10 {~cm}{/tex} and 14 cm
height {tex}=5 {~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a parallelogram depends on
questions.explanation: Area = base × height.

questions.question: Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 12 cm and 10 cm.
questions.explanation:
Use the formula for the area of a rhombus:
Area {tex}=\frac{1}{2} d_1 d_2{/tex}
Substitute the values:

{tex}\text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 10{/tex}
Area {tex}=60 {~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: A diagonal of a rectangle divides it into two triangles. These triangles
questions.explanation: Each triangle is half of the rectangle.

questions.question:
The area of a parallelogram is 96 cm².
If its base is 12 cm, its height is

questions.question: Find the area of a triangle whose base is 10 cm and height is 8 cm.

= ½ × 10 × 8 = 40 cm²

questions.question: Two rectangles have the same perimeter. Which of the following is always true?
questions.explanation: Same perimeter does not guarantee same area.

questions.question: The area of a trapezium with parallel sides 8 cm and 12 cm and a height of 5 cm is
questions.explanation:

questions.question: The area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 10 cm and 6 cm is

questions.question: The formula for the area of a triangle with base 'b' and height 'h' is

questions.question: A rectangle is divided into two congruent triangles by drawing its diagonal. The area of each triangle is

options.option: equal to the area of the rectangle

questions.explanation:
A diagonal of a rectangle divides it into two equal (congruent) triangles.

Each triangle has:

{tex} \frac{1}{2} \times \text { area of rectangle }{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a trapezium is 84 cm2. If its height is 7 cm and one parallel side is 10 cm, find the other parallel side.
questions.explanation:

{tex}98=7 x{/tex}
{tex}x=14{/tex}

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezium are 12 cm and 8 cm, and its area is 100 cm2. Find its height.
questions.explanation:

{tex}h=10{/tex}

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezium are 10 cm and 6 cm, and its height is 5 cm. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Area of a trapezium:

{tex}a=10, b=6, h=5{/tex}
{tex}\text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times(10+6) \times {/tex}{tex}5=\frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times 5=40{/tex}

Answer: {tex}40 \ {cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: If the diagonals of a rhombus are equal, the rhombus is a ________.
questions.explanation:
In a rhombus, all sides are equal.
If its diagonals are also equal, then it becomes a square because:

  • Equal diagonals in a rhombus imply all angles are {tex}90^{\circ}{/tex}.

So the figure is a square.

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are in the ratio 3 : 4. If its area is 216 cm2, find the lengths of the diagonals.
questions.explanation:
Let diagonals be {tex}3 x{/tex} and {tex}4 x{/tex}.
Area of rhombus:

{tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 3 x \times 4 x=216{/tex}
{tex}6 x^2=216{/tex}
{tex}x^2=36 \Rightarrow x=6{/tex}

So diagonals:

{tex} 3 x=18 {~cm}, 4 x=24 {~cm}{/tex}

Answer: 18 cm and 24 cm

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are 12 cm and 16 cm. Its area is ________ cm2.
questions.explanation:
Area of a rhombus:

{tex} \text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times d_1 \times d_2{/tex}

Given:

{tex}d_1=12, d_2=16{/tex}
{tex}\text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 16=96{/tex}

Answer: {tex}96 \ {cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a parallelogram with base 12 cm and height 8 cm is ________ cm2.
questions.explanation:
Area of a parallelogram:

{tex} \text { Area }=\text { base} \times \text {height }{/tex}

Given:

{tex} 12 \times 8=96{/tex}

Answer: {tex}96 \ {cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The length and breadth of a rectangle are in the ratio {tex}3: 2{/tex}. If its area is {tex}150 \ {cm}^2{/tex}, find its length.
questions.explanation:
Let length {tex}=3 x{/tex}, breadth {tex}=2 x{/tex}
Area:

{tex}3 x \times 2 x=150{/tex}
{tex}6 x^2=150{/tex}
{tex}x^2=25 \Rightarrow x=5{/tex}

Length:

{tex} 3 x=15 {~cm}{/tex}

questions.question: The base of a triangle is 20 cm, and its height is 12 cm. Find its area.
questions.explanation:
Area of a triangle:

{tex} \text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times \text { base} \times \text{height }{/tex}

Given:

{tex}\text { Base }=20, \text { Height }=12{/tex}
{tex}\text { Area }=\frac{1}{2} \times 20 \times 12=120{/tex}

Answer: {tex}120 {~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The side of a square is 15 cm. Its area is ________ cm2.
questions.explanation:
Area of a square:

{tex} \text { Area }=\text { side }^2{/tex}

Given side {tex}=15 {~cm}{/tex}:

{tex} 15^2=225{/tex}

Answer: {tex}225 {~cm}^2{/tex}

questions.question: The area of a rectangle is 96 cm2. If its length is 12 cm, what is its breadth?
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangle:

{tex} \text { Area }=\text { length } \text { × } \text { breadth }{/tex}

Given:

{tex} 96=12 \times b{/tex}

So,

{tex} b=\frac{96}{12}=8{/tex}
